context: Tests whether the agent correctly applies scope naming conventions — using the module or directory name as scope, keeping it lowercase and hyphenated, and omitting scope entirely when a change spans multiple modules. Five commits cover single-module and cross-cutting changes.

checklist.name: Scope present — Commit A
checklist.description: Commit A (auth-only change) includes a scope, e.g., `feat(auth):` or `fix(auth):`

checklist.name: Scope uses directory name — Commit A
checklist.description: The scope in Commit A matches the module/directory name `auth` (not a description like `authentication` or `login`)

checklist.name: Scope present — Commit B
checklist.description: Commit B (db-only change) includes a scope, e.g., `fix(db):`

checklist.name: Scope uses directory name — Commit B
checklist.description: The scope in Commit B matches the module/directory name `db` (not `database` or `data-layer`)

checklist.name: No scope — Commit C
checklist.description: Commit C (multi-area change: deps, config, CI) does NOT include a scope — the type prefix has no parenthesized scope

checklist.name: Scope present — Commit D
checklist.description: Commit D (task-queue only change) includes a scope

checklist.name: Scope hyphenated — Commit D
checklist.description: The scope for Commit D is `task-queue` (hyphenated, matching the directory name) — NOT `taskqueue`, `task_queue`, or `TaskQueue`

checklist.name: Scope lowercase — all messages
checklist.description: Every scope used across all five commits is entirely lowercase — no uppercase letters appear inside the parentheses

checklist.name: No scope — Commit E
checklist.description: Commit E (spans user-profile and notifications modules) does NOT include a scope

checklist.name: Conventional format preserved
checklist.description: All five rewritten messages still follow the `type(scope): description` or `type: description` format — no free-form messages
